description: Baseline for chimp128 runtime using just bench-target; add autoresearch.md/autoresearch.sh/autoresearch.checks.sh infrastructure
Optimize chimp128 encode/decode ring-buffer indexing: replace iterator-based fallback scan with manual bounded loop and use bitmask (&127) for modulo-128 indexing
Reduce chimp128 lookup overhead: use u32 lookup table in encode and remove unused decode lookup table writes
Speed up bin helpers used by chimp128 by replacing tiny-array iteration/position lookups with direct match-based bin_count/bin_encode/bin_decode
Tested forcing inline(always) on chimp bin helper functions; benchmark regressed so reverted
Tried switching decode ring-buffer safety assert! to debug_assert! to remove runtime check overhead; no improvement over best
Replace chimp128 encode fallback ring-buffer scan with O(1) previous-value reference when hash lookup misses, removing per-sample 128-entry search
Attempted removing encode lookup table entirely and always using previous ring entry reference; faster but broke chimp128 golden vector encoding compatibility
Minor hot-loop cleanup: iterate encode input as `for &curr in &input[1..]` to avoid repeated dereferencing, plus retain updated autoresearch notes
Tried preallocating decode output with Vec::with_capacity(count); benchmark regressed on target workload
Tested zero-initializing chimp128 encode ring buffer now that lookup-miss path no longer scans sentinels; no improvement
Optimize bitstream hot paths: add byte-aligned fast paths in put_u64_lowest_bits/read_u64_lowest_bits and inline bit operations in src/bits.rs
Reduce chimp128 encode leading-zero overhead by using new bin_leading_and_code(xor) helper to compute bin count and 3-bit code in one match
Tried wrapping_sub-based lookup age check in chimp128 encode; compile failed due ambiguous integer type for index
Retried chimp128 lookup age check with wrapping_sub and explicit usize index; benchmark near best but correctness checks failed (gorilla property test), so discarded
Fix gorilla correctness for high-leading-zero xors by capping encoded leading-zero count to 31 (5-bit field); checks now stable and chimp128 benchmark also improved
Retested replacing chimp128 decode assert! with debug_assert! after bitstream optimizations; still slightly worse than best
Tried reusing a precomputed chimp128 lookup key (`(curr_bits as usize) & 0x3FFF`) to avoid duplicate mask/cast; regressed
Add dedicated aligned 64-bit fast paths in bit I/O: use put_u64 for writes and from_be_bytes slice read for count==64 when bit-aligned
Tried unsafe unaligned u64 read in aligned count==64 Bitread fast path to avoid byte copy; regressed benchmark
Retested preallocating chimp128 decode output Vec with capacity(count); still slower on target benchmark
Tried table-based bin_decode array indexing instead of match; slight regression vs current best
Removed chimp128 decode ring-buffer initialization assert check entirely; no improvement versus best
Tried preallocating chimp128 encode BytesMut with input.len()*8 capacity to avoid growth reallocations; no gain
Tested forcing inline(always) for hot bitstream methods after adding aligned fast paths; no further improvement
Tried 0-sentinel lookup encoding (store index+1) in chimp128 encode to avoid u32::MAX checks; significantly slower
Tried index-based while loop for chimp128 encode input traversal instead of slice iterator; regressed
Tried while-loop traversal in chimp128 decode to reduce iterator overhead; slightly slower than best
Re-tested chimp128 encode lookup table as usize (vs u32) after other optimizations; clearly slower
Tried generic byte-multiple fast paths (count%8==0) in bit read/write using to_be_bytes/copy_from_slice; significantly slower
Tested forcing inline(always) for bin_leading_and_code helper used in chimp128 encode; regressed
Tried omitting trailing zero flush byte when bitstream ends on byte boundary in Bitwrite::into_bytes; breaks golden encoded byte compatibility across codecs
Retested zero-initializing chimp128 encode ring buffer with current optimized bit paths; regressed heavily
Tried using slice try_into for aligned 64-bit Bitread fast path instead of copy_from_slice; slower
Speed up small bit-field operations in Bitread/Bitwrite by adding single-byte fit fast paths for read_u64_lowest_bits/put_u64_lowest_bits (hot for chimp128 control fields)
Attempted thread-local generation-stamped lookup table reuse for chimp128 encode to avoid per-call 16k clear; initial implementation failed borrow checker
Implement thread-local generation-stamped lookup cache for chimp128 encode to avoid reinitializing 16,384-entry lookup array each call
Tested replacing thread-local RefCell lookup state with UnsafeCell to remove borrow checks; regressed benchmark
Tried chimp128-specific bit-width wrapper methods (put_u3/u6/u7 and read_u3/u6/u7) to specialize control-field I/O; regressed
Tried packed u64 lookup entries (epoch|index) for thread-local chimp128 cache to reduce struct field loads; regressed
Retried preallocating chimp128 encode output buffer (BytesMut::with_capacity(input.len()*8)) on new baseline; still regressed
Tried explicit count==1 fast paths in generic bit read/write methods; worsened branch behavior and regressed runtime
Re-evaluated chimp128 decode output preallocation on current baseline: return early with Vec::new for empty and use Vec::with_capacity(count) for decode results
Retested zero-initializing chimp128 encode ring buffer after lookup-cache/preallocation changes; still regressed
Tried precomputed BIT_MASKS lookup table for small-width bit read/write mask generation; regressed
Inline chimp128 decode bin lookup by replacing bin_decode() calls with direct 8-entry leading-zero table indexing on 3-bit codes
Tried hoisting chimp decode bin table to module-level const CHIMP_BINS instead of inline literals; regressed
Tried unsafe decode output prefill via Vec::with_capacity + set_len + indexed writes to avoid push overhead; slightly slower than best
Refactor generic bit read/write loops to align-first byte-block processing (unaligned head bits, branchless byte chunk loop, tail bits), cutting per-iteration branching overhead
Further optimize bit I/O unaligned head handling by consuming initial partial byte in one packed operation (both write and read) before byte-chunk loops
Eliminate per-bit tail loops in bit read/write: handle final sub-byte remainder with single packed operation (write pending bitbuf directly, read from current byte with mask/shift)
Tried narrowing chimp128 decode best_index to usize directly to remove cast in ring lookup; regressed badly on benchmark
Retried unsafe decode prefill (Vec::set_len + indexed writes) on new bitstream baseline; clearly slower than push-based decode
Optimize chimp128 encode lookup access by avoiding per-iteration LookupEntry copy; read epoch/value directly from entries[key]
Tried mutating lookup entry fields in-place instead of assigning a LookupEntry struct in chimp128 encode; regressed
Tried replacing chimp128 decode bin table indexing with explicit match on 3-bit leading code; regressed
Force inline(always) on hot bitstream primitives (put_bit/put_u64_lowest_bits/read_bit/read_u64_lowest_bits) after loop refactors; large win
Tried adding inline(always) to put_f64/read_f64 wrappers on top of inlined bit primitives; regressed
Retested inline(always) for bin_leading_and_code after latest bitstream wins; still slightly slower
Retested changing chimp128 decode assert to debug_assert after recent optimizations; regressed
Speed up mid-sized bit transfers by adding 32-bit chunk loops in put_u64_lowest_bits/read_u64_lowest_bits (use put_u32/from_be_bytes before byte/tail handling)
Attempted adding 16-bit chunk fast paths in bit read/write; edit introduced malformed src/bits.rs braces (compile error), reverted
Force inline(always) for remaining hot bitstream methods (put_bit, put_u64_lowest_bits, read_bit) on top of read_u64 inlining and chunked loops
Tried unsafe unaligned u32 reads in read_u64_lowest_bits 32-bit chunk loop to avoid copy_from_slice; regressed
Tried unsafe get_unchecked/get_unchecked_mut for chimp128 lookup table accesses (key masked to 14 bits); regressed
Attempted adding layered 32/16-bit chunk loops in bit read/write; broad text replacement corrupted src/bits.rs test module braces
Add wider chunk paths in bit transfer core: 32-bit block writes in put_u64_lowest_bits plus 16-bit block reads in read_u64_lowest_bits after 32-bit chunks
Add 16-bit block writes in put_u64_lowest_bits (after 32-bit chunk loop) to reduce byte-loop iterations for mid-sized payloads
Tried dedicated aligned count==32 read/write fast paths in bitstream core in addition to chunk loops; slightly regressed
Add true small-width specialized bit ops (put_small/read_small const-generic for <=8 bits) and switch chimp128 hot 3/6/7-bit control fields to these paths
Attempted splitting chimp128 thread-local lookup cache into separate epochs/values arrays; initial version failed borrow checker due simultaneous mutable borrows
Refactor chimp128 generation-stamped lookup cache to structure-of-arrays (epochs + values) and access via single RefCell borrow in loop
Tried unsafe unaligned u16 loads in read_u64_lowest_bits 16-bit chunk loop to avoid copy_from_slice; slight regression
Tested shrinking chimp128 lookup epoch metadata from u32 to u16 to reduce cache footprint; no improvement
Control rerun with no code changes to estimate benchmark noise on current best branch; observed higher metric this run
Tried branch hinting with std::hint::likely on chimp128 trailing-threshold branch; unavailable on stable toolchain (unstable feature)
Retested forcing inline(always) on put_bit and put_u64_lowest_bits after small-width specialization and SoA lookup refactor; regressed
Tried destructuring lookup_state fields into local epochs/values slices to reduce repeated field dereference in chimp128 encode loop; regressed
Retested removing chimp128 decode ring-buffer initialization assert in current optimized baseline; clear regression
Attempted manual byte-assembly in read_u64 chunk loops (replace from_be_bytes/copy_from_slice); broad text replacement corrupted trailing src/bits.rs braces
Reorder chimp128 encode branch by making trailing<=13 path the first condition (likely case on noisy patterns), preserving semantics with inverted if/else layout
Tried reordering chimp128 decode top-level branch to handle control-bit=1 path first (mirroring likely encode branch); regressed
Tried manual 4-byte assembly in read_u64_lowest_bits 32-bit chunk loop (avoid copy_from_slice/from_be_bytes); regressed
Tried replacing thread-local RefCell lookup cache with UnsafeCell to remove borrow overhead; implementation broke imports/type inference and failed to compile
Retested inline(always) on put_f64/read_f64 wrappers with latest baseline; slightly slower
Tried explicitly caching lookup_state.epochs[key] into local before epoch compare in chimp128 encode loop; substantial regression
